messages.content: The ______ of a wave is proportional to the amount of energy the wave carries.
The amplitude of a wave is proportional to the amount of energy the wave carries.
A _______ wave consisits of a series of regularly spaced wave pulses.
A periodic wave consists of a series of regularly spaced wave pulses.
The ______ is the highest point of a wave.
The crest is the highest point of a wave.
Waves transmit ______ from one place to another.
Waves transmit energy from one place to another.
The number of wave cycles that occur per unit time is known as the _______.
The number of wave cycles that occur per unit time is known as the frequency.
A ________ wave is one in which the particles of the medium oscillate back and forth in the same direction in which the wave is traveling.
A longitudinal wave is one in which the particles of the medium oscillate back and forth in the same direction in which the wave is traveling.
A ________ wave is a wave that requires a medium to travel through.
A mechanical wave is a wave that requires a medium to travel through.
The substance through which a mechanical wave travels is known as the ______.
The substance through which a mechanical wave travels is known as the medium.
The amount of time required for one complete wave cycle is known as the ______.
The amount of time required for one complete wave cycle is known as the period.
A wave _____ is a single cycle of energy sent through a medium.
A wave pulse is a single cycle of energy sent through a medium.
The frequency of a wave is determined by the ______ of the wave.
The frequency of a wave is determined by the source of the wave.
A type of wave that exhibits properties of both longitudinal and transverse waves is known as a ______ wave.
A type of wave that exhibits properties of both longitudinal and transverse waves is known as a surface wave.
In a ______ wave, the particles of the substance through which the wave moves oscillate perpendicularly to the direction the wave travels.
In a transverse wave, the particles of the substance through which the wave moves oscillate perpendicularly to the direction the wave travels.
The lowest point on a wave is called a ______.
The lowest point on a wave is called a trough.
The ______ is the distance between the adjacent crests of a wave.
The wavelength is the distance between adjacent crests of a wave.
A _______ wave consisits of a series of regularly spaced wave pulses.
A periodic wave consists of a series of regularly spaced wave pulses.
